violations.text: No person certified in food safety was present as needed at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2024
A pitcher was observed held at the hand sink in the kitchen. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2024

Sausage was observed held at 103 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Inspector determined sausage was made within the hour, staff re-heated food at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2024
Boxes of food were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2024
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine. New Violation. Correct By: 11/05/2024

The following non-food contact surfaces were observed with an accumulation of debris: 1) The fan covers in the walk-in cooler had biofilm 2) The shelves in the walk-in cooler had biofilm New Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2024
A pitcher was observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Item was moved to the warewashing area at the time of visit. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2023

There is insufficient refrigeration equipment/capacity to maintain a temperature of 41 degrees or below in the walk-in refrigerator due to the cooling of potatoes. Walk-in cooler ambient observed at 49 degrees Fahrenheit due to volume of hot foods in a cooling process. Items removed from refrigeration to other refrigeration/ice bath to properly cool. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2023

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Soup was placed hot placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. Person in charge moved items to a shallow pan/ice bath to properly cool. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2023

Reduced oxygen packaged salmon was observed in the walk-in cooler at 41 degrees Fahrenheit with the package intact. Person in charge voluntarily disposed of food items. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2023
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2023
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine in the kitchen. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine has an accumulation biofilm. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2023
